title: Paraphrase evasion of keyword guard
description: Tests whether PUB-008's overclaim can be rephrased with synonyms so that v0.2's keyword-based negative_space guard fails to flag the contradiction.

oracle_origin.author: dispatch
oracle_origin.context_exposure: full — author of both artifact and fixture

test_cases.input: negative_space entry references 'structural model'; digest contains 'structural_model' keyword

test_cases.description: Synonym substitution — 'architectural framework' instead of 'structural model'
test_cases.input: negative_space entry references 'structural model'; digest uses 'architectural framework' for same concept
test_cases.expected: keyword guard should flag semantic equivalence but likely won't
test_cases.notes: This is the known weakness. If it passes, the guard is better than expected.

test_cases.description: Conceptual paraphrase — entirely different vocabulary for same claim
test_cases.input: negative_space limits scope to 'pattern recognition in data structures'; digest claims 'we identified recurring motifs in information architectures'
test_cases.expected: keyword guard has zero lexical overlap — cannot catch this
test_cases.notes: Pure paraphrase. Only semantic analysis could catch this. Current v0.2 has no semantic layer.

interpretation.if_all_pass: Keyword guard is stronger than expected — requires revision of this fixture's assumptions
interpretation.if_B_fails: Expected behavior. Guard catches keywords, not semantics. PUB-012 must explicitly declare this limitation.
interpretation.if_all_fail: Guard is even weaker than assumed. PUB-012's scope_limits are more binding than the profile's own text admits.
